edges.description: Reduced ZEB2 function perturbs brain development programs.
Reduced ZEB2 function perturbs developmental programs relevant to Hirschsprung disease.
Impaired brain development produces early global developmental delay.
Persistent neurodevelopmental impairment contributes to intellectual disability.
Abnormal cortical development and network maturation predispose to epilepsy.
Enteric developmental defects produce congenital aganglionic megacolon in a substantial subset of patients.

nodes.description: Developmental disruption downstream of ZEB2 contributes to the high frequency of Hirschsprung disease and related gastrointestinal manifestations in Mowat-Wilson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mowat-Wilson syndrome: unraveling the complexities of diagnosis, treatment, and symptom management
evidence.snippet: The severity of the disease and associated signs and symptoms can vary widely but may include distinct facial features, developmental delay, intellectual disability, and Hirschsprung.

nodes.description: ZEB2 haploinsufficiency disrupts neurodevelopmental programs and underlies the severe developmental and epileptic phenotype of Mowat-Wilson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mowat-Wilson syndrome: unraveling the complexities of diagnosis, treatment, and symptom management
evidence.snippet: The severity of the disease and associated signs and symptoms can vary widely but may include distinct facial features, developmental delay, intellectual disability, and Hirschsprung.

nodes.description: Anti-seizure medications are commonly used in Mowat-Wilson syndrome, with levetiracetam and valproic acid showing benefit in the reported epilepsy cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Electro-clinical features of Mowat-Wilson syndrome: A retrospective study of 31 children in mainland China.
evidence.snippet: Thirty received ASMs; both levetiracetam and valproic acid, used as monotherapy or in combination, proved effective.

nodes.description: Global developmental delay is a core and usually early feature of Mowat-Wilson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mowat-Wilson syndrome: unraveling the complexities of diagnosis, treatment, and symptom management
evidence.snippet: The severity of the disease and associated signs and symptoms can vary widely but may include distinct facial features, developmental delay, intellectual disability, and Hirschsprung.

nodes.description: Hirschsprung disease is a classic congenital anomaly associated with Mowat-Wilson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mowat-Wilson syndrome: unraveling the complexities of diagnosis, treatment, and symptom management
evidence.snippet: The severity of the disease and associated signs and symptoms can vary widely but may include distinct facial features, developmental delay, intellectual disability, and Hirschsprung.

nodes.description: Moderate to severe intellectual disability is a defining clinical feature of Mowat-Wilson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mowat-Wilson syndrome: unraveling the complexities of diagnosis, treatment, and symptom management
evidence.snippet: The severity of the disease and associated signs and symptoms can vary widely but may include distinct facial features, developmental delay, intellectual disability, and Hirschsprung.

nodes.description: Epilepsy is common in Mowat-Wilson syndrome and often begins in early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Electro-clinical features of Mowat-Wilson syndrome: A retrospective study of 31 children in mainland China.
evidence.snippet: Among the 31 children (16 males and 15 females), seizure onset occurred at a median age of 25.5 months (range: 1-113 months).

evidence.reference_title: Electro-clinical features of Mowat-Wilson syndrome: A retrospective study of 31 children in mainland China.
evidence.snippet: All 31 children had de novo ZEB2 variants, including 27 with single-nucleotide variants (SNVs) or insertions/deletions (indels) and four with copy number variants.
ZEB2 | HGNC:14881 | Mowat-Wilson syndrome | MONDO:0009341 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Mowat-Wilson syndrome is caused by pathogenic ZEB2 variants that reduce the dosage of this developmental transcription factor and disrupt downstream embryonic patterning programs.

evidence.reference_title: Electro-clinical features of Mowat-Wilson syndrome: A retrospective study of 31 children in mainland China.
evidence.snippet: All 31 children had de novo ZEB2 variants, including 27 with single-nucleotide variants (SNVs) or insertions/deletions (indels) and four with copy number variants.
